county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Sherman County formed 1873, organized 1886. County seat is Goodland. Named for General William T. Sherman. Far-northwestern Kansas on the Colorado border. Union Pacific Railroad corridor. Dust Bowl effects. Sparsely populated agricultural county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Sherman County Register of Deeds in Goodland holds land records from the 1880s. A far- northwestern Kansas county on the Colorado border. The Sherman County Historical Society and Museum in Goodland has local collections. BLM GLO land patents document homestead entries. Probate Court records from the 1880s. Church records from pioneer Catholic, Methodist, and Lutheran congregations. Goodland newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. Union Pacific Railroad records at KSHS may document workers and land sales. Dust Bowl effects were significant. The county was a Dust Bowl resettlement area.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Sherman County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Goodland

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Smith County formed 1872, organized 1872. County seat is Smith Center. Named for Major J. Nelson Smith, killed in Civil War. Geographic center of the contiguous United States (near Lebanon). North-central Kansas bordering Nebraska. Solomon River valley.
county_metadata.research_tips: Smith County Register of Deeds in Smith Center holds land records from the 1870s. The geographic center of the contiguous United States is in Smith County (near Lebanon). The Smith County Historical Society has local collections. BLM GLO land patents document homestead entries. Scandinavian and German settlers left church records from the 1870s-1880s. Probate Court records from the 1870s. Smith Center newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed some Smith County records. The county borders Nebraska; cross-border family research may be needed. The Solomon River valley was the primary settlement area.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Smith County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Smith Center

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Stafford County formed 1879. County seat is St. John. Named for Captain Lewis Stafford, killed in Civil War. Central Kansas wheat farming county. Quivira National Wildlife Refuge. German-Russian and Mennonite settlers.
county_metadata.research_tips: Stafford County Register of Deeds in St. John holds land records from 1879. The Stafford County Historical Society and Museum in St. John has local collections. BLM GLO land patents document homestead entries. German-Russian and Mennonite settlers left church records from the 1880s. Probate Court records from 1879. St. John newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed some Stafford County records. Quivira National Wildlife Refuge occupies part of the county. The county had significant wheat farming records. District Court naturalization records at the courthouse.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Stafford County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in St. John

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Stanton County formed 1887 from Hamilton County. County seat is Johnson City. Named for Edwin M. Stanton, Secretary of War. Far-southwestern Kansas. One of the most sparsely populated Kansas counties. Extreme Dust Bowl devastation. Natural gas development.
county_metadata.research_tips: Stanton County Register of Deeds in Johnson City holds land records from 1887. Formed from Hamilton County -- check Hamilton County records for earlier entries. One of the most sparsely populated Kansas counties. The Stanton County Historical Society has local family files. BLM GLO land patents are essential for tracing homesteaders. Probate Court records from 1887. Church records from pioneer congregations are sparse. Johnson City Pioneer newspaper at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. Dust Bowl devastation was extreme. Natural gas development brought later families.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Stanton County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Johnson City

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Stevens County formed 1886. County seat is Hugoton. Named for Thaddeus Stevens, Reconstruction-era congressman. Violent county seat war between Hugoton and Woodsdale in the 1880s. Hugoton Gas Field (largest in North America). Southwestern Kansas.
county_metadata.research_tips: Stevens County Register of Deeds in Hugoton holds land records from 1886. The county experienced a violent county seat war between Hugoton and Woodsdale in the 1880s. The Stevens County Historical Society and Gas Museum in Hugoton has local collections. BLM GLO land patents. Probate Court records from 1886. Church records from pioneer congregations. Hugoton Hermes newspaper at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. Natural gas production (Hugoton Gas Field, largest in North America) brought workers. Dust Bowl effects were severe. Mexican American agricultural workers settled from the mid-20th century.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Stevens County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Hugoton

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Sumner County formed 1867 from Butler County. County seat is Wellington. Named for Senator Charles Sumner of Massachusetts. Borders Oklahoma (Cherokee Outlet). Wellington was the last major Kansas cattle market. Caldwell was a border town. Mennonite and Exoduster settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Sumner County Register of Deeds in Wellington holds land records from 1867. The county borders Oklahoma and was adjacent to the Cherokee Outlet. Wellington was the last major Kansas cattle market. The Sumner County Historical Society and Museum in Wellington has local genealogical collections. BLM GLO land patents. Diverse immigrant communities (German, Mennonite, African American Exodusters) left church records from the 1870s. Probate Court records from 1867. Wellington newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed Sumner County records. Caldwell was a border town with Indian Territory; town marshal records at KSHS.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Sumner County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Wellington

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Thomas County formed 1873, organized 1885. County seat is Colby. Named for General George H. Thomas, Civil War officer. Northwestern Kansas. Union Pacific Railroad corridor. Dust Bowl effects. Prairie Museum of Art and History. Sparsely populated.
county_metadata.research_tips: Thomas County Register of Deeds in Colby holds land records from the 1880s. A northwestern Kansas county. The Thomas County Historical Society and Prairie Museum of Art and History in Colby has local collections. BLM GLO land patents document homestead entries. Probate Court records from the 1880s. Church records from pioneer Catholic and Methodist congregations. Colby newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. Dust Bowl effects caused outmigration. Union Pacific Railroad records at KSHS may document workers. German- Russian settlers left church records at local parishes.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Thomas County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Colby

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Trego County formed 1867, organized 1879. County seat is WaKeeney. Named for Captain Edgar P. Trego, killed at Chickamauga. WaKeeney was a planned colony by Chicago investors. German-Russian Catholic settlers. Cedar Bluff Reservoir. Western Kansas.
county_metadata.research_tips: Trego County Register of Deeds in WaKeeney holds land records from the 1880s (practical settlement). WaKeeney was a planned colony founded by Chicago investors. The Trego County Historical Society and Museum in WaKeeney has local collections. BLM GLO land patents. German-Russian Catholic settlers left parish records from the 1870s-1880s. Probate Court records from the 1880s. WaKeeney newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. The county is named for Captain Edgar P. Trego, killed at Chickamauga. Cedar Bluff Reservoir construction affected some communities.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Trego County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in WaKeeney

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Wabaunsee County formed 1855 as an original Kansas Territory county. County seat is Alma. Named for Chief Wabaunsee of the Pottawatomie. Strong German heritage (Alma, Paxico). Beecher Bible and Rifle Church documents the Free State movement. Flint Hills region. Mill Creek valley.
county_metadata.research_tips: Wabaunsee County Register of Deeds in Alma holds land records from 1855. The county has a strong German heritage, particularly around Alma and Paxico. The Wabaunsee County Historical Society and Museum in Alma has local genealogical collections. BLM GLO land patents. German Lutheran and Catholic church records from the 1850s-1860s are important. Beecher Bible and Rifle Church in Wabaunsee documents the Free State movement -- abolitionist records at KSHS. Probate Court records from 1855. Alma newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed Wabaunsee County records. The Flint Hills region has ranching records. Mill Creek valley was the primary settlement corridor.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Wabaunsee County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Alma

source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival from 1855; deed books are substantially complete

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Wallace County formed 1868, organized 1888. County seat is Sharon Springs. Named for General W.H.L. Wallace, killed at Shiloh. Far-western Kansas on the Colorado border. Fort Wallace (1865-1882). Sparsely populated. Dust Bowl effects. Fort Wallace Museum.
county_metadata.research_tips: Wallace County Register of Deeds in Sharon Springs holds land records from the 1880s. A far- western Kansas county on the Colorado border. The Wallace County Historical Society has local family files. BLM GLO land patents are essential for tracing homesteaders. Fort Wallace (1865-1882) military records at NARA document soldiers and early settlers. Probate Court records from the 1880s. Church records from pioneer congregations are sparse. Sharon Springs newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. The county experienced severe Dust Bowl effects. The Fort Wallace Museum preserves local history.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Wallace County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Sharon Springs

source_profiles.source_name: Wallace County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (homestead, preemption, timber culture, cash entry) for Wallace County; Kansas is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

source_profiles.source_name: Wallace County Military Records -- Fort Wallace / NARA / KSHS
source_profiles.repository: NARA / Kansas State Historical Society, Topeka
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Military service records, pension files, muster rolls for Wallace County residents; Fort Wallace post records at NARA; Kansas adjutant general records at KSHS; Civil War, Indian Wars, Spanish-American War, and WWI/WWII records

source_profiles.source_name: Wallace County Marriage Records -- Probate Court / KSHS / FamilySearch
source_profiles.repository: Wallace County Probate Court, Sharon Springs / Kansas State Historical Society, Topeka
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and records for Wallace County from 1868; Probate Court issued marriage licenses in Kansas; KSHS holds some county marriage records on microfilm; FamilySearch has indexed Kansas marriages; state vital records registration began 1911
